sourceMindset: List-driven and identity-driven cinephile who logs everything and treats the watchlist as a curated canon.
survivalInstinct: Protects taste depth and rejects anything that feels like mainstream popularity wearing a cinephile costume.
patienceLevel: High for depth; low for being condescended to.
trustRequirement: Specific, grounded reasons that name real taste seeds and avoid fake authority.
abandonmentTrigger: Picks feel mainstream, reasons are generic, or FeelFlick feels like a worse Letterboxd.
returnTrigger: One surprising deep-cut pick that proves the engine read actual taste.
onboardingIntent: Seed loved films that define taste canon and check whether FeelFlick respects deep cuts.
notes: Use to stress whether a recommendation has taste literacy without pretending to replace logging, lists, or diary workflows.

sourceMindset: Fact-first evaluator who checks cast, crew, runtime, ratings, vote counts, and metadata before deciding.
survivalInstinct: Distrusts arbitrary scores and asks whether every claim can be backed by a checkable signal.
patienceLevel: Medium; willing to inspect evidence but not hand-waving.
trustRequirement: Reasons and match signals must cite something verifiable and make clear what numbers mean.
abandonmentTrigger: Match percentages feel inflated, arbitrary, or unsupported by the visible evidence.
returnTrigger: A pick whose reasoning is transparent enough to verify and agree with.
onboardingIntent: Provide broad seeds and then scrutinize whether the product turns data into a decision honestly.
notes: Use to pressure-test score labeling, rationale precision, and whether metadata supports the recommendation rather than decorating it.

sourceMindset: Tired evening viewer who browses until exhausted and often gives up or rewatches something safe.
survivalInstinct: Avoids setup, niche homework, and anything that turns into another grid to evaluate.
patienceLevel: Low; wants to be watching within minutes.
trustRequirement: The pick must feel right quickly, low-effort, and not pretentious.
abandonmentTrigger: Onboarding is long, the pick feels random or too arty, or the experience becomes another scroll surface.
returnTrigger: One good pick removes the scroll and gets them watching.
onboardingIntent: Give just enough signal to get a safe and immediate Tonight pick.
notes: Use to test whether FeelFlick actually reduces decision fatigue for low-patience casual viewers.

sourceMindset: Availability-first household chooser who will not invest in a recommendation that cannot be watched tonight.
survivalInstinct: Avoids dead ends where a great recommendation has no clear way to press play.
patienceLevel: Medium; practical but allergic to wasted effort.
trustRequirement: The product must answer or honestly handle where the film can be watched.
abandonmentTrigger: FeelFlick recommends unavailable films and never says where or why availability is missing.
returnTrigger: A pick that is clearly watchable tonight within their subscriptions or availability expectations.
onboardingIntent: Look for whether the recommendation path respects access constraints without becoming an availability app.
notes: Use to stress the boundary between useful availability context and drifting into a where-to-watch replacement.

sourceMindset: Systematic tracker who expects history, stats, progress, and watch state to matter.
survivalInstinct: Rejects products that ignore watched history or repeatedly recommend films already seen.
patienceLevel: High for tracking; low for tools that ignore logged behavior.
trustRequirement: Must not re-recommend watched films and must show that history improves future picks.
abandonmentTrigger: Repeated watched picks, stale history awareness, or missing tracking expectations.
returnTrigger: A pick that clearly reflects logged history and proves the system learns.
onboardingIntent: Seed a coherent watched history and inspect whether it affects recommendations and DNA.
notes: Use to test compounding behavior and the line between taste history and full tracker replacement.

sourceMindset: Design-literate viewer who values polish, craft, restraint, and an editorial point of view.
survivalInstinct: Leaves when the interface feels cheap, cluttered, overhyped, or generically written.
patienceLevel: Medium; tolerant of considered experiences but not jank.
trustRequirement: Polished presentation, credible writing, honest case-making, and no fake hype.
abandonmentTrigger: The product looks unfinished, over-promises, or makes a thin editorial case.
returnTrigger: The Film File and recommendation feel considered and tastefully presented.
onboardingIntent: Assess whether first impressions and recommendation writing feel editorially credible.
notes: Use to evaluate product craft and whether restraint strengthens trust.

sourceMindset: Mainstream low-effort viewer who wants familiar, safe entertainment without a lot of setup.
survivalInstinct: Avoids niche, arty, difficult, or high-commitment choices.
trustRequirement: The pick must feel familiar enough to be safe and require almost no effort.
abandonmentTrigger: Picks feel obscure or onboarding asks too much before proving value.
returnTrigger: A no-brainer pick that is enjoyable and easy to accept.
onboardingIntent: Move through onboarding quickly and judge whether the first recommendation feels safe.
notes: Use to test whether FeelFlick can serve mainstream convenience without becoming another generic grid.

sourceMindset: Tech-savvy media owner who values library control, ownership, transparency, and configurability.
survivalInstinct: Rejects walled-garden assumptions and cloud-only products that ignore personal collections.
trustRequirement: Clear controls, transparent scope, and honest handling of unsupported personal-library workflows.
abandonmentTrigger: FeelFlick assumes only streaming behavior or hides the absence of library integration.
returnTrigger: A pick that fits taste and a product boundary that is honest about library support.
onboardingIntent: Probe controls, preferences, and whether personal-library expectations are acknowledged gracefully.
notes: Use to test how gracefully FeelFlick says not yet to personal-library integration.

sourceMindset: Visual, impulse-led chooser who decides from trailers, clips, posters, and immediate vibe.
survivalInstinct: Bails when the experience is text-heavy, slow, or visually boring.
trustRequirement: The recommendation must look exciting within seconds; a trailer or strong visual hook helps.
abandonmentTrigger: Too much reading, slow explanation, or a pick that looks boring.
returnTrigger: An instant visual hook that makes the pick feel exciting.
onboardingIntent: Move quickly and judge whether the product communicates vibe without a long text burden.
notes: Use to test whether visual evidence supports fast decision-making without turning the app into clip browsing.

sourceMindset: Trusts people and authentic reasons more than algorithmic claims or marketing copy.
survivalInstinct: Hunts for fabricated hype, fake social proof, and soulless generic recommendations.
trustRequirement: An honest, human-sounding reason grounded in taste, with no fabricated social proof.
abandonmentTrigger: Claims like people like you without basis, generic marketing language, or invented hype.
returnTrigger: A pick and rationale that feel repeatable to a friend.
onboardingIntent: Seed enough taste to check whether FeelFlick can make a human-feeling case.
notes: Use to pressure-test generated authority, social proof, and reason authenticity.

sourceMindset: Exhausted nightly viewer who wants not to decide and needs the product to read mood without extra work.
survivalInstinct: Protects limited evening energy and abandons anything that feels like a survey or chore.
patienceLevel: Low for setup; high for something that finally gets the mood.
trustRequirement: The pick must feel like it understood tonight's mood and the reason must ring true.
abandonmentTrigger: The app makes them work or the pick ignores how they feel.
returnTrigger: Mood in, one pick out, watched with no fatigue.
onboardingIntent: Give minimal taste and mood signals, then test whether the app ends the scroll.
notes: Core target persona for the primary user problem: a trusted pick that ends decision fatigue.

sourceMindset: Open-minded streamer who wants to discover personal taste without film-school vocabulary or condescension.
survivalInstinct: Rejects fake flattery, random picks, and personalization that overstates what it knows.
trustRequirement: Cinematic DNA must feel true and picks must feel personal without being generic.
abandonmentTrigger: DNA feels fake, flattering, or picks feel random.
returnTrigger: The product reflects something real about taste and stretches just enough.
onboardingIntent: Seed accessible favorites and see whether the product explains taste in a useful way.
notes: Core target persona for taste discovery without condescension.

sourceMindset: Two-person shared-sofa decision maker trying to break the nightly what do we watch standoff.
survivalInstinct: Avoids anything that restarts negotiation or appears to model only one person's taste.
trustRequirement: One pick neither person vetoes, with a reason both can accept.
abandonmentTrigger: The product models only one person or turns the decision back into a debate.
returnTrigger: It picked, both said yes, and the negotiation ended.
onboardingIntent: Seed overlap tastes and evaluate whether a single-user pick still helps a shared decision.
notes: Use to surface the multi-user gap while testing whether the single-pick loop can still reduce household friction.

archetype: Serious film lover who hates algorithmic slop
sourceMindset: Quality-gated film lover burned by recommendation engines and dishonest popularity bias.
survivalInstinct: Rejects mediocre popular picks, fabricated claims, fake social proof, and recency bias instantly.
patienceLevel: High for quality; zero for slop.
trustRequirement: Specific true reasons, genuinely good picks, anti-recency discipline, and no fabricated authority.
abandonmentTrigger: One fabricated claim, one mediocre popular pick, or one fake social-proof cue.
returnTrigger: A recommendation that feels real, honest, and not insulting.
onboardingIntent: Seed serious taste and pressure-test whether the product stays honest under high standards.
notes: Harshest honesty persona; use to test the trust moat under adversarial quality expectations.

archetype: New user with almost no logged history
sourceMindset: New arrival with thin data who needs value before commitment and honesty about uncertainty.
survivalInstinct: Leaves if the app demands too much data or pretends to know them deeply too soon.
patienceLevel: Low; must see value before committing.
trustRequirement: Honest cold-state language and a plausible first pick without fake confidence.
abandonmentTrigger: Fake confidence, fabricated DNA, or a random first pick.
returnTrigger: A believable first pick plus honest still-learning framing.
onboardingIntent: Provide minimal seeds and inspect whether the product creates a credible cold-start experience.
notes: Core target persona for the cold-to-warm trust jump.

archetype: Returning user with enough history for Cinematic DNA
sourceMindset: Returning user with enough logged history to expect the product to compound and feel personal.
survivalInstinct: Protects the ritual from stale DNA, repeated picks, and recommendations that stop using depth.
patienceLevel: Medium; expects payoff from having fed the system.
trustRequirement: Picks visibly use history, DNA feels accurate, and tonight does not repeat yesterday.
abandonmentTrigger: Repeated picks, stale DNA, or recommendations that stop feeling personal.
returnTrigger: The app feels better because it knows more now.
onboardingIntent: Represent a warmed-up account with enough history to test compounding behavior.
notes: Payoff persona for long-term reusable beta accounts and repeated regression-style walkthroughs.

sourceMindset: Neutral baseline account for setup checks, auth-state verification, and workflow debugging.
survivalInstinct: Keeps persona-specific findings separate from tooling and environment failures.
trustRequirement: The account should be clearly labeled synthetic and never treated as a persona finding source.
abandonmentTrigger: Used as evidence for UX conclusions or mixed into persona-specific reports.
returnTrigger: Provides a stable control path for confirming provisioning and auth storage workflows.
onboardingIntent: Reserved for shared QA setup and control checks, not synthetic persona walkthrough conclusions.
notes: Shared/control account. Do not use as a synthetic beta persona or real-user validation source.
